narrative:
DOMINANT DRIVERS

1. USD structural weakness because DXY soft at 98.21, USD proxy at -0.33, and broad commodity strength confirm sustained dollar selling pressure
2. AUD and GBP currency strength because CSM ranks AUD at 100 and GBP at 85, leading the risk-on rotation with high conviction across correlated pairs
3. Risk-on regime because equity gains, copper up 1.0%, silver up 3.94%, and geopolitical de-escalation are collectively suppressing safe-haven demand for USD, JPY, and CHF

PRICE CONFIRMS

AUDUSD and NZDUSD correlation at 0.94 confirms commodity currency outperformance is broad-based rather than pair-specific, with AUD leading and NZD following. USDCHF sell signal on D1 aligns with the USDCHF-EURUSD negative correlation of -0.71, confirming CHF strength is driven by USD weakness rather than independent safe-haven demand. EURJPY and GBPJPY both showing H4 sell signals while USDJPY holds suggest JPY is the laggard in this move, with cross-JPY weakness confirming the yen is gaining versus European currencies despite the risk-on backdrop.

REGIME

Risk-On confirmed. VIX at 16.99 is elevated but not spiking, S&P 500 up 0.29% at all-time highs, and Gold up 0.65% reflects dollar debasement buying rather than fear, consistent with the regime classification.

CONTRADICTIONS

1. Gold and risk-on: Gold rising 0.65% to 4644 alongside equities conflicts with classic risk-on logic but is resolved by the USD weakness narrative driving both simultaneously
2. VIX up 0.59% with equities also rising: minor divergence suggesting equity complacency may be building, though insufficient to override the confirmed risk-on alignment

TRADE IMPLICATIONS

Best: AUDUSD up - AUD strongest CSM, USD weakest proxy, D1 and H4 regime aligned - Conviction 8/10
Secondary: USDCHF down - D1 sell confirmed, USD weakness dominant, CHF benefits from dollar selling - Conviction 7/10
Avoid: EURJPY - D1 neutral, H4 sell, EUR CSM at zero creates conflicting cross pressure
Watch: DXY breaking below 98.00 confirms USD breakdown and validates AUDUSD long as primary trade
key_observation: Broad-based commodity rally across agricultural and energy sectors coinciding with dollar weakness and record equity highs suggests sustained risk-on environment. Multiple commodity themes point to USD underperformance.
watch: Dollar index weakness continuation, equity momentum sustainability, crude oil trajectory following Iran negotiations commentary, commodity rally breadth persistence
